title: Trying Out
prose: Second-years can, technically, try out for the house team — it's just that they essentially never make it, not with sixth- and seventh-years also competing for the same handful of spots. You go anyway, brooms borrowed from the school supply, a knot of nerves in your stomach as the captain runs everyone through drills on the pitch.

You do well enough — a decent catch, a clean loop, nothing disastrous — but the two spots open this year go to older, more experienced players, and the list posted the next morning doesn't have your name on it.
It stings for about a day. Then it mostly just becomes a reason to fly for fun instead, with nobody's expectations riding on it.

choices.label: Decide you'll try again next year.
Decide flying for its own sake is enough.
